steps.title: Use the intercept and slope as two points
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is $(0,-5)$. The slope is $2$, so moving right $1$ raises the output by $2$, giving a second point $(1,-3)$.

steps.title: Fit the line
steps.content: Type $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. Desmos returns $m=2$ and $b=-5$, so $f(x)=2x-5$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice D is the only equation with slope $2$ and $y$-intercept $-5$. Therefore choice D is correct.
